content_en:
Abdullah Ibn Hudhafah As-Sahmi Scanned from: "Companions of The Prophet", Vol.1, By: Abdul Wahid Hamid. History would have by-passed this man as it had bypassed thousands of Arabs before him. He, like them, would have had no claim to attention or fame. The greatness of Islam, however, gave to Abdullah ibn Hudhafah the opportunity to meet two world potentates of his timeÑKhusraw Parvez the King of Persia and Heraclius, the Byzantine emperor. The story of his encounter with Khusraw Parvez began in the sixth year of the hijrah when the Prophet decided to send some of his Companions with letters to rulers outside the Arabian peninsula inviting them to Islam. The Prophet attached great importance to this initiative. These messengers were going to distant lands with whom there was no agreement or treaty. They did not know the languages of these lands nor anything about the ways and disposition of their rulers. They were to invite these rulers to give up their religion and forsake their power and glory and enter the religion of a people who shortly before were almost their subjects. The mission was undoubtedly hazardous . To make known his plan, the Prophet called his companions together and addressed them. He started by praising God and thanking Him. He then recited the Shahadah and went on: "I want to send some of you to the rulers of foreign lands but don't dispute with me as the Israelites disputed with Jesus, the son of Mary. "O Prophet of God, we shall carry out whatever you wish," they responded. "Send us wherever you desire." The Prophet commissioned six of his Sahabah to carry his letters to Arab and foreign rulers. One of these was Abdullah ibn Hudhafah. He was chosen to take the Prophet's letter to Khusraw Parvez, the Persian king. Abdullah got his camel ready and bade farewell to his wife and son. He set out, alone, and traversed mountains and valleys until he reached the land of the Persians. He sought permission to enter into the king's presence informing the guards of the letter he was carrying. Khusraw Parvez thereupon ordered his audience chamber to be made ready and summoned his prominent aides. When they had assembled he gave permission for Abdullah to enter. Abdullah entered and saw the Persian potentate dressed in delicate, flowing robes and wearing a great, neatly arranged turban. On Abdullah was the plain, coarse clothes of the bedouin. His head though was held high and his feet were firm. The honour of Islam burned fiercely in his breast and .he power of faith pulsated in his heart. As soon as Khusraw Parvez saw him approaching he signalled to one of his men to take the letter from

his hand. "No," said Abdullah. "The Prophet commanded me to hand over this letter to you directly and I shall not go against a command of the Messenger of God." "Let him come near to me," Khusraw said to his guards and Abdullah went forward and handed over the letter. Khusraw then called an Arab clerk who originally came from Hira and ordered him to open the letter in his presence and read its contents. He began reading: "In the name of Allah, the Beneficent the Merciful. From Muhammad, the Messenger of God, to Khusraw the ruler of Persia. Peace on whoever follows the guidance . . ." Khusraw only heard this much of the letter when the fire of anger burst within him. His face became red and he began to perspire around the neck. He snatched the letter from the clerk's hand and began tearing it to pieces without knowing what else it contained and shouted, "Does he dare to write to me like this, he who is my slave"? He was angry that the Prophet had not given him precedence in his letter. He then commanded Abdullah to be expelled from his assembly. Abdullah was taken away, not knowing what would happen to him. Would he be killed or would he be set free? But he did not want to wait to find out. He said, "By God, I don't care what happens to me after the letter of the Prophet has been so badly treated." He managed to get to his camel and rode off. When Khusraw's anger had subsided he commanded that Abdullah be brought before him. But Abdullah was nowhere to be found. They searched for him all the way to the Arabian peninsula but found that he had gone ahead. Back in Madinah, Abdullah told the Prophet how Khusraw had torn his letter to pieces and the Prophet's only reply was, "May God tear up his kingdom". Meanwhile, Khusraw wrote to Badhan, his deputy in the Yemen, to send two strong men to "that man who has appeared in the Hijaz" with orders to bring him to Persia. Badhan despatched two of his strongest men to the Prophet and gave them a letter to him in which he was ordered to go with the two men to meet Khusraw without delay. Badhan also asked the two men to get whatever information they could on the Prophet and to study his message closely. The men set out, moving very quickly. At Ta'if they met some Quraysh traders and asked them about Muhammad. "He is in Yathrib," they said and they went on to Makkah feeling extremely happy. This was good news for them and they went around telling other Quraysh, "You will be pleased. Khusraw is out to get Muhammad and you will be rid of his evil." The two men meanwhile made straight for Madinah where they met the Prophet, handed him the letter of Badhan and said to him, "The king of kings, Khusraw, has written to our ruler Badhan to send his men to get you. We have come to take you with us. If you come willingly, Khusraw has said that it will be good for you and he will spare you any punishment. If you refuse, you will know the power of his punishment. He has power to destroy you and your people." The Prophet smiled and said to them, "Go back to your mounts today and return tomorrow." On the following day, they came to the Prophet and said to him, "Are you prepared to go with us to meet Khusraw?"

"You shall not meet Khusraw after today," replied the Prophet. "God has killed him and his son Shirwaih has taken his place on such a night and on such a month." The two men stared in the face of the Prophet. They were completely dumbfounded. "Do you know what you are saying?" they asked. "Shall we write about this to Badhan?" "Yes," replied the Prophet, "and say to him that my religion has informed me about what has happened to the kingdom of Khusraw and that if he should become Muslim, I would appoint him ruler over what he now controls". The two men returned to the Yemen and told Badhan what had happened. Badhan said, "If what Muhammad has said is true, then he is a Prophet. If not then we shall see what happens to him." Not long afterwards, a letter from Shirwaih came to Badhan in which he said, "I killed Khusraw because of his tyranny against our people. He regarded as lawful the killing of leaders, the capturing of their women and the expropriating of their wealth. When this my letter reaches you, take the allegiance of whoever is with you on my behalf." As soon as Badhan had read Shirwaih's letter, he threw it aside and announced his entry into Islam. The Persians with him in the Yemen also became Muslim. That's the story of Abdullah ibn Hudhafah's meeting with the Persian king. His meeting with the Byzantine emperior took place during the caliphate of Umar ibn alKhattab. It too is an astonishing story. In the nineteenth year after the Hijrah, Umar despatched an army to fight against the Byzantines. In it was Abdullah ibn Hudhafah. News of the Muslim force reached the Byzantine emperior. He had heard of their sincerity of faith, and their willingness to sacrifice their lives in the way of God and His Prophet. He gave orders to his men to bring to him any Muslim captive they might take alive. God willed that Abdullah ibn Hudhafah should fall captive to the Byzantines and he was brought before the Emperor. The Emperor looked at Abdullah for a long time. Suddenly he said, "I shall make a proposal to you." "What is it?" asked Abdullah. "I suggest that you become a Christian. If you do this, you will be set free and I shall grant you a safe refuge." The prisoner's reaction was furious: "Death is preferable to me a thousand times to what you ask me to do." "I see that you are a bold man. However, if you respond positively to what I propose to you, I will give you a share in my authority and swear you in as my aide." The prisoner, shackled in his chains, smiled and said, "By God, if you give me all that you possess and all that the Arabs have in exchange for giving up the religion of Muhammad, I shall not do so." "Then I shall kill you." "Do what you want," answered Abdullah.

The emperor then had him put on a cross and ordered his soldiers to throw spears at him, first near his hands and then near his feet, all the while telling him to accept Christianity or at least give up his religion. This he refused over and over again to do. The emperor then had him taken down from the wooden cross. He called for a great pot to be brought. This was filled with oil which was then heated under a fierce fire. He then had two other Muslim prisoners brought and had one of them thrown into the boiling oil. The prisoner's flesh sizzled and soon his bones could be seen. The emperor turned to Abdullah and invited him to Christianity. This was the most terrible test that Abdullah had had to face up till now. But he remained firm and the emperor gave up trying. He then ordered that Abdullah too be thrown into the pot. As he was being taken away he began to shed tears. The emperor thought that he had at last been broken and had him brought back to him. He once more suggested that Abdullah become a Christian but to his astonishment, Abdullah refused. "Damn you! Why did you weep then?" shouted the emperor. "I cried," said Abdullah, "because I said to myselfÑ 'You will now be thrown into this pot and your soul will depart'. What I really desired then was to have as many souls as the number of hairs on my body and to have all of them thrown into this pot for the sake of God." The tyrant then said, "Will you kiss my head? I will then set you free?" "And all the Muslim prisoners also?" asked Abdullah. This the emperor agreed to do and Abdullah said to himself, "One of the enemies of God! I shall kiss his head and he shall set me and all other Muslim prisoners free. There can be no blame on me for doing this." He then went up to the emperor and kissed his forehead. All the Muslim prisoners were released and handed over to Abdullah. Abdullah ibn Hudhafah eventually came to Umar ibn alKhattab and told him what had happened. Umar was greatly pleased and when he looked at the prisoners he said, "Every Muslim has a duty to kiss the head of Abdullah ibn Khudhafah and I shall start." Umar then got up and kissed the head of Abdullah ibn Hudhafah. Scanned from: "Companions of The Prophet", Vol.1, By: Abdul Wahid Hamid.
content_id:
Sejarah mungkin akan melewatkan orang ini sebagaimana ia melewatkan ribuan orang Arab sebelumnya. Seperti mereka, ia tidak akan memiliki klaim atas perhatian atau ketenaran. Namun keagungan Islam memberikan kepada Abdullah ibn Hudzafah (ra) kesempatan untuk bertemu dua penguasa dunia di zamannya: Khusraw Parvez, Raja Persia, dan Heraklius, Kaisar Byzantium. Kisah pertemuannya dengan Khusraw Parvez dimulai pada tahun keenam Hijrah ketika Nabi ﷺ memutuskan untuk mengutus beberapa sahabatnya dengan surat-surat kepada para penguasa di luar Semenanjung Arabia, mengundang mereka untuk masuk Islam. Nabi ﷺ sangat mementingkan inisiatif ini. Para utusan ini akan pergi ke negeri-negeri jauh yang tidak ada perjanjian atau kesepakatan dengan mereka. Mereka tidak mengetahui bahasa negeri-negeri itu maupun adat istiadat penguasanya. Mereka diutus untuk mengajak para penguasa itu meninggalkan agama dan kekuasaan mereka serta masuk ke dalam agama suatu kaum yang sebelumnya hampir menjadi bawahan mereka. Misi ini jelas penuh bahaya.

Untuk menyampaikan rencananya, Nabi ﷺ mengumpulkan para sahabat dan berpidato kepada mereka. Beliau memulai dengan memuji Allah dan bersyukur kepada-Nya. Kemudian beliau membaca syahadat dan melanjutkan: "Aku ingin mengutus beberapa dari kalian kepada para penguasa negeri-negeri asing, tetapi janganlah kalian mendebatku sebagaimana Bani Israil mendebat Isa putra Maryam." "Wahai Nabi Allah, kami akan melaksanakan apa pun yang engkau kehendaki," jawab mereka. "Utuslah kami ke mana pun engkau inginkan." Nabi ﷺ menugaskan enam sahabatnya untuk membawa surat-suratnya kepada para penguasa Arab dan asing. Salah satu di antaranya adalah Abdullah ibn Hudzafah (ra). Beliau dipilih untuk membawa surat Nabi ﷺ kepada Khusraw Parvez, Raja Persia.

Abdullah menyiapkan untanya dan berpamitan dengan istri dan putranya. Beliau berangkat seorang diri, melintasi pegunungan dan lembah hingga tiba di tanah Persia. Beliau meminta izin untuk menghadap raja sambil memberitahukan bahwa beliau membawa surat. Khusraw Parvez pun memerintahkan agar ruang audiensinya disiapkan dan memanggil para pembesar kedekatannya. Ketika mereka sudah berkumpul, ia memberikan izin kepada Abdullah untuk masuk. Abdullah masuk dan melihat penguasa Persia itu berpakaian jubah halus yang mengalir dan memakai sorban besar yang tersusun rapi. Sementara itu, Abdullah mengenakan pakaian kasar orang Badui. Namun kepalanya tegak dan kakinya kokoh berpijak. Kehormatan Islam membara dengan kuat di dadanya dan kekuatan iman berdenyut di hatinya. Begitu Khusraw Parvez melihatnya mendekat, ia memberi isyarat kepada salah seorang pengawalnya untuk mengambil surat dari tangannya. "Tidak," kata Abdullah. "Nabi ﷺ memerintahkanku untuk menyerahkan surat ini langsung kepadamu, dan aku tidak akan menyalahi perintah Rasulullah ﷺ." "Biarkan dia mendekat," kata Khusraw kepada pengawalnya, dan Abdullah pun maju dan menyerahkan surat itu. Khusraw kemudian memanggil seorang juru tulis Arab dari Hirah dan memerintahkannya untuk membuka surat itu di hadapannya dan membaca isinya. Dia mulai membaca: "Bismillahirrahmanirrahim. Dari Muhammad, Rasulullah ﷺ, kepada Khusraw, penguasa Persia. Salam kepada orang yang mengikuti petunjuk..." Khusraw baru mendengar sejauh ini ketika amarah meledak di dalam dirinya. Wajahnya memerah dan ia mulai berkeringat di sekitar leher. Ia merenggut surat itu dari tangan juru tulis dan mulai merobeknya berkeping-keping tanpa mengetahui apa lagi isinya, lalu berteriak: "Beraninya dia menulis kepadaku seperti ini, dia yang merupakan hambaku?" Ia marah karena Nabi ﷺ tidak mendahulukannya dalam suratnya. Kemudian ia memerintahkan agar Abdullah diusir dari majelisnya.

Abdullah dibawa pergi tanpa mengetahui apa yang akan terjadi padanya. Namun beliau tidak ingin menunggu untuk mengetahuinya. Beliau berkata: "Demi Allah, aku tidak peduli apa yang akan terjadi padaku setelah surat Nabi ﷺ diperlakukan sedemikian buruk." Beliau berhasil mencapai untanya dan melarikan diri. Ketika amarah Khusraw mereda, ia memerintahkan agar Abdullah dibawa kembali ke hadapannya. Namun Abdullah tidak ditemukan di mana pun. Mereka mencarinya hingga ke Semenanjung Arabia tetapi mendapati bahwa beliau telah pergi lebih dulu. Kembali di Madinah, Abdullah menceritakan kepada Nabi ﷺ bagaimana Khusraw merobek suratnya, dan Nabi ﷺ hanya menjawab: "Semoga Allah merobek-robek kerajaannya." Sementara itu, Khusraw menulis kepada Badhan, wakilnya di Yaman, untuk mengirim dua orang kuat kepada "orang yang muncul di Hijaz" dengan perintah untuk membawanya ke Persia. Badhan mengutus dua orang terkuatnya kepada Nabi ﷺ dan memberi mereka surat yang memerintahkan beliau ﷺ untuk pergi bersama keduanya menemui Khusraw tanpa penundaan. Badhan juga meminta keduanya untuk mendapatkan informasi apa pun yang mereka bisa tentang Nabi ﷺ dan mempelajari risalahnya secara seksama.

Kedua orang itu berangkat dengan cepat. Di Taif mereka bertemu para pedagang Quraisy dan menanyakan tentang Muhammad ﷺ. "Beliau ada di Yathrib," jawab mereka, dan keduanya melanjutkan ke Makkah dengan perasaan sangat gembira. Berita itu terasa seperti kabar baik bagi mereka. Kedua orang itu kemudian langsung menuju Madinah, menemui Nabi ﷺ, menyerahkan surat Badhan, dan berkata: "Raja segala raja, Khusraw, telah menulis kepada tuan kami Badhan untuk mengirimkan orang-orangnya untuk menjemputmu. Kami telah datang untuk membawamu bersama kami. Jika engkau datang dengan sukarela, Khusraw berkata hal itu akan baik bagimu dan ia akan mengampunimu dari hukuman. Jika engkau menolak, engkau akan mengetahui kekuatan hukumannya. Ia punya kuasa untuk menghancurkan engkau dan kaummu." Nabi ﷺ tersenyum dan berkata kepada mereka: "Kembalilah ke tunggangan kalian hari ini dan kembali lagi besok." Keesokan harinya, mereka datang kepada Nabi ﷺ. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepada mereka: "Kalian tidak akan bertemu Khusraw setelah hari ini. Allah telah membunuhnya dan putranya Shirwaih telah menggantikannya di malam yang demikian dan pada bulan yang demikian." Kedua orang itu terpaku dan terperanjat. "Apakah engkau mengetahui apa yang engkau katakan?" tanya mereka. "Apakah kami boleh menulis ini kepada Badhan?" "Ya," jawab Nabi ﷺ, "dan katakanlah kepadanya bahwa agamaku telah memberitahuku tentang apa yang telah terjadi pada kerajaan Khusraw dan bahwa jika ia menjadi Muslim, aku akan mengangkatnya sebagai penguasa atas apa yang sekarang dikuasainya."

Kedua orang itu kembali ke Yaman dan menceritakan kepada Badhan apa yang telah terjadi. Badhan berkata: "Jika apa yang Muhammad katakan itu benar, maka dia adalah seorang Nabi. Jika tidak, kita akan lihat apa yang akan terjadi padanya." Tidak lama kemudian, sepucuk surat dari Shirwaih datang kepada Badhan yang isinya: "Aku membunuh Khusraw karena kekejamannya terhadap rakyat kami. Ia menganggap halal membunuh para pemimpin, menawan perempuan mereka, dan merampas kekayaan mereka. Ketika suratku ini sampai kepadamu, ambillah kesetiaan dari siapa pun yang bersamamu atas namaku." Begitu Badhan selesai membaca surat Shirwaih, ia melemparkannya dan mengumumkan penerimaannya terhadap Islam. Orang-orang Persia yang bersamanya di Yaman pun ikut masuk Islam. Itulah kisah pertemuan Abdullah ibn Hudzafah (ra) dengan Raja Persia.

Pertemuannya dengan Kaisar Byzantium terjadi pada masa kekhalifahan Umar ibn al-Khattab (ra). Pada tahun kesembilan belas setelah Hijrah, Umar (ra) mengirimkan satu pasukan untuk berperang melawan Byzantium. Abdullah ibn Hudzafah (ra) termasuk di dalamnya. Ia tertangkap oleh pasukan Byzantium dan dibawa ke hadapan Kaisar. Kaisar menatapnya lama, lalu tiba-tiba berkata: "Aku akan mengajukan sebuah usulan kepadamu." "Apa itu?" tanya Abdullah. "Aku menyarankan agar kamu menjadi Kristen. Jika kamu melakukan ini, kamu akan dibebaskan dan aku akan memberikanmu perlindungan." Reaksi sang tawanan itu keras: "Kematian seribu kali lebih aku sukai daripada apa yang kamu minta aku lakukan." "Aku melihat bahwa kamu adalah orang yang berani. Namun jika kamu merespons positif terhadap apa yang aku usulkan, aku akan memberikan kamu bagian dalam otoritasku dan menyumpahmu sebagai pembantuku." Sang tawanan, dibelenggu rantai-rantainya, tersenyum dan berkata: "Demi Allah, seandainya kamu memberikan segalanya yang kamu miliki dan semua yang dimiliki orang-orang Arab kepadaku sebagai ganti dari meninggalkan agama Muhammad ﷺ, aku tidak akan melakukannya." "Kalau begitu aku akan membunuhmu." "Lakukan apa yang kamu mau," jawab Abdullah.

Kaisar kemudian memintanya dipasang di kayu salib dan memerintahkan prajuritnya untuk melemparkan tombak-tombak ke arahnya, pertama dekat tangannya lalu dekat kakinya, sambil terus memintanya untuk menerima Kristen atau setidaknya meninggalkan agamanya. Hal ini berulang kali ia tolak. Kaisar kemudian memintanya diturunkan dari kayu salib dan memanggil sebuah kuali besar yang diisi minyak dipanaskan di atas api yang menyala-nyala. Dua tahanan Muslim lain dibawa, dan salah satunya dilemparkan ke dalam minyak mendidih. Daging tahanan itu mendesis dan tidak lama kemudian tulang-tulangnya terlihat. Kaisar berpaling kepada Abdullah dan mengajaknya menjadi Kristen. Ini adalah ujian paling mengerikan yang harus dihadapi Abdullah sampai saat itu. Namun beliau tetap teguh. Ketika Abdullah kemudian akan dilemparkan ke dalam kuali, beliau mulai menangis. Kaisar mengira beliau akhirnya telah goyah dan menyuruh membawa beliau kembali. Ia sekali lagi menyarankan Abdullah menjadi Kristen, namun dengan terperanjat, Abdullah menolak. "Sialan kamu! Mengapa kamu menangis kalau begitu?" bentak Kaisar. "Aku menangis," kata Abdullah, "karena aku berkata kepada diriku sendiri: 'Kamu sekarang akan dilemparkan ke dalam kuali ini dan nyawamu akan pergi.' Yang benar-benar aku inginkan saat itu adalah memiliki nyawa sebanyak jumlah rambut di tubuhku dan semuanya dilemparkan ke dalam kuali ini demi Allah."

Sang tiran kemudian berkata: "Ciumlah kepalaku? Aku akan membebaskanmu?" "Dan semua tahanan Muslim juga?" tanya Abdullah. Kaisar setuju, dan Abdullah berkata kepada dirinya sendiri: "Musuh Allah ini! Aku akan mencium kepalanya dan dia akan membebaskanku dan semua tahanan Muslim lainnya. Tidak ada celaan bagiku untuk melakukan ini." Ia kemudian mendekati Kaisar dan mencium dahinya. Semua tahanan Muslim dibebaskan dan diserahkan kepada Abdullah. Abdullah ibn Hudzafah (ra) akhirnya menghadap Umar ibn al-Khattab (ra) dan menceritakan apa yang terjadi. Umar (ra) sangat gembira dan ketika menatap para tahanan itu berkata: "Setiap Muslim berkewajiban untuk mencium kepala Abdullah ibn Hudzafah dan aku akan memulainya." Umar kemudian bangkit dan mencium kepala Abdullah ibn Hudzafah (ra).
content_ar:
عبد الله بن حذافة السهمي. كان التاريخ ليمر بهذا الرجل مر الكرام كما مر بآلاف العرب قبله، ولم يكن ليطمح إلى شهرة أو ذكر. غير أن عظمة الإسلام أتاحت لعبد الله بن حذافة فرصة نادرة أن يلتقي بملكين من عظماء الدنيا في زمانه: كسرى أبرويز ملك فارس، وهرقل إمبراطور الروم. وقد بدأت قصة لقائه بكسرى في العام السادس للهجرة حين عزم النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم على إرسال عدد من الصحابة بكتب إلى حكام خارج شبه الجزيرة العربية يدعونهم إلى الإسلام.

وقد اختار النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم عبد الله بن حذافة حاملاً كتابه إلى كسرى ملك الفرس. فجهّز راحلته وودّع زوجه وولده، ثم انطلق وحيداً يجتاز الجبال والوديان حتى بلغ ديار الفرس. واستأذن في الدخول على الملك مُعرِّفاً بنفسه ومُبلِّغاً إياه رسالة النبي. فلما قرأ كسرى الكتاب مزّقه أمامه غاضباً ومتكبراً. فلما بلغ النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ذلك قال: "مزّق الله ملكه".

ولم تنته مآثر عبد الله بن حذافة عند هذا؛ فقد وقع لاحقاً في أسر الروم في إحدى الغزوات، فعرض عليه الإمبراطور هرقل الارتداد عن الإسلام مقابل الحرية والثروة والمنزلة الرفيعة، فأبى إباءً قاطعاً. فلما رأى هرقل صموده وإيمانه الراسخ أعجبه شأنه وأفرج عنه، وأوفد معه أسرى مسلمين كثيرين تكريماً لتلك النفس الأبيّة. وقد أخبر عمر بن الخطاب بقصته فقام إليه وقبّله في جبهته.
